evidence.q1: Sections 1–18 are numbered sequentially. Cross-reference example: "all recovery for Section 13 or Section 14 claims is limited to the amount stated in Section 16" (Section 18) resolves to Confidentiality (13), Third-Party Claims (14), and Liability (16).
evidence.q2: Defined terms in Section 2 (Customer Data, Deliverables, Vendor Methods, Security Incident) are defined once and used consistently. Order Form defined in Section 1 also used consistently.
evidence.q3: All time and quantity terms are specific, e.g., "within thirty days" (Section 4), "within 72 hours" (Section 7), "forty-five days" (Section 9), "one year" (Section 10). No vague phrasing like "reasonable while" appears.
evidence.q4: No archaic legal diction. Examples: "Vendor will provide hosted records…" uses modern "will," not "shall." No hereby/herein/whereas.
evidence.q5: Section 1 contains a broken/redundant phrase: "Vendor will provide hosted records normalization, normalization, mapping, and workflow services…" (repetition of "normalization" disrupts sentence coherence).
evidence.q6: Register is uniform across sections. For instance, Section 15 uses same modern, direct tone as Section 5: active voice, "will" usage, no stylistic shift.
